L1_Muyldermans2013_23495938.topic: VHH general review — heavy-chain-only antibodies, VHH structure, applications
L1_Muyldermans2013_23495938.specific_contribution: Defines VHH structural hallmarks (pos44/45/47) and their role in solubility. Covers VH vs VHH differences. No humanization data.
L1_Muyldermans2013_23495938.limitation: Review article — synthesizes literature rather than providing original data on humanization outcomes

L2_Vincke2009_19010777.topic: VHH humanization experimental study — CAbLys3 humanization with 3 plans
L2_Vincke2009_19010777.specific_contribution: The DEFINITIVE humanization reference. Provides: (1) 4 hallmark positions (42,49,50,52 in Chothia = 37,44,45,47 in Kabat/Mitchell); (2) Result: 49→G improves stability but reduces solubility;     52→W is DETRIMENTAL (affinity loss); (3) Universal humanized scaffold (Plan A = 3DWT, Plan B = 3EAK). PDB structures 3DWT + 3EAK are now in our database.
L2_Vincke2009_19010777.limitation: Only 1 VHH target (anti-lysozyme CAbLys3). 3 humanization plans. No immunogenicity data.

L3_Mitchell2018_29569425.topic: Comparative sequence/structure analysis — 90 VHH crystal structures
L3_Mitchell2018_29569425.specific_contribution: Largest systematic analysis of VHH at time of publication. Key data: (1) FR regions are MORE conserved in VHH than VH; (2) CDR1/H1 and CDR2/H2 show greater structural diversity than VH counterparts; (3) H3 loop (CDR3) is longer on average; (4) VHH tetrad positions 37,44,45,47 composition analysis.
L3_Mitchell2018_29569425.limitation: Uses non-humanized VHH structures (camelid-derived). Does NOT include humanized sequences. JOURNAL WAS WRONG: Proteins, not PLoS Comput Biol.

L4_Spinelli1996_8784347.topic: Crystal structure of llama heavy chain variable domain (structural letter)
L4_Spinelli1996_8784347.specific_contribution: One of the first structural observations of VHH domain. Short letter — minimal mechanistic data.
L4_Spinelli1996_8784347.limitation: 1996 letter — no sequence analysis. Superseded by L5 (Desmyter 1996) and L3 (Mitchell 2018).

L5_Desmyter1996_8784355.topic: Crystal structure of camel VHH in complex with lysozyme — 2.5Å
L5_Desmyter1996_8784355.specific_contribution: First full structural analysis of VHH. Key finding: FR2 region that contacts VL in conventional VH is RESHAPED in VHH to increase hydrophilicity. CDR3 is long and folds OVER the VL-contact surface. This provides structural rationale for why pos44/45/47 are hydrophilic in VHH.
L5_Desmyter1996_8784355.limitation: Single camelid VHH — Camelus dromedarius. No humanization data.

L6_North2011_21035459.topic: CDR loop clustering analysis — >300 antibody structures
L6_North2011_21035459.specific_contribution: Canonical CDR loop clusters for H1/H2/H3. Used for CDR fold type classification (H1-8, H2-8-1 etc.) in R4.
L6_North2011_21035459.limitation: Focused on conventional VH/VL — VHH CDR loops not explicitly classified. Rule R4 uses this for CDR fold type only.

L7_Spinelli2000_10684599.topic: VHH structure against hapten (RR6 azo dye) — CDR loop contributions
L7_Spinelli2000_10684599.specific_contribution: Shows FR3 Vernier zone and CDR loop interactions. CDR1/CDR2/CDR3 all contribute to hapten binding. Structural rationale for FR3 Vernier positions.
L7_Spinelli2000_10684599.limitation: Hapten binding — different from protein antigen. No humanization data.
